raw.title: Wars destroy lives and the climate. Why aren’t we counting military emissions?
raw.excerpt: War is a major driver of greenhouse gas emissions, yet most conflict-related emissions remain excluded from official climate accounting. Governments and international climate bodies must begin treating military emissions and the climate costs of war as central issues of accountability and justice.

llm_processed.summary: Military emissions are significant contributors to greenhouse gas emissions and should be counted in climate discussions.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: This could enable a more comprehensive understanding of emissions sources and inform policy decisions to reduce them.
